headline_summary: IEA chief says Middle East oil supply disruptions will rise in April as Strait of Hormuz closure bites; diesel and jet fuel shortages flagged (Reuters, Apr 1)
Bahrain circulates revised UN Security Council draft on protecting shipping in/around Strait of Hormuz; China, Russia and France raise issues (Reuters, Apr 1)
US LNG exports hit record in March as Middle East war disrupts global supply and takes ~20% of global LNG supply offline (Reuters, Apr 1)
Trump says US will be out of Iran 'pretty quickly' and could return for 'spot hits'; NATO frictions noted (Reuters, Apr 1)
Dubai crude benchmark faces stress as Hormuz export halts disrupt deliverable grades and pricing mechanisms (Reuters, Apr 1)
Kenya tea exports hit as shipping disruption forces reroutes and leaves stocks piling up in Mombasa (Reuters, Apr 1)
Oil prices slide after Trump de-escalation comments, but uncertainty over reopening supply chains persists (Reuters, Apr 1)
OPEC oil output plunges in March as Hormuz closure forces export cuts, Reuters survey finds (Reuters, Mar 31)
Explosions heard in Damascus attributed by Syrian state TV to Israel intercepting Iranian missiles (Reuters, Mar 31)
Israel expands operations in southern Lebanon; Lebanon braces for long-term displacement crisis (Reuters, Mar 29-31)
risk_drivers: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively shut/near-halted; contested rules for safe passage
No UN consensus yet on authorizing maritime protection; escalation risk at sea remains elevated
Refined-products (diesel/jet) shortages and LNG outages add inflation and growth risks
Potential second chokepoint exposure at Bab al-Mandab if Yemen's Houthis target Red Sea shipping
Cross-border drone/missile incidents across Gulf/Levant keep tail-risk high for energy and logistics infrastructure
Benchmark/market-function stress (Dubai crude pricing) increases volatility and hedging dislocations

Energy: Crude and refined products retain upside risk premium while Hormuz is constrained; diesel/jet cracks and LNG spreads likely stay elevated (Reuters, Apr 1).
Volatility increases due to policy headlines (UN draft, US drawdown rhetoric) vs physical constraints (OPEC export cuts, benchmark disruption).
FX: Risk-off plus higher energy import bills can pressure oil-importer EM FX; USD tends to benefit during acute escalation (Reuters context: energy shock).
Rates: Energy-driven inflation risk supports breakevens; higher uncertainty can widen credit spreads and keep front-end rate cuts priced more cautiously.
Equities: Energy/defense beneficiaries vs airlines, shipping, chemicals and energy-intensive industries facing margin pressure; broader EM equity risk premium elevated.

sources.title: Middle East oil supply disruptions to rise in April and hit Europe, IEA chief says
Bahrain's Hormuz resolution runs into fresh obstacles at UN
US LNG exports break record high as Middle East war disrupts global supply
US to leave Iran 'pretty quickly' and return if needed, Trump tells Reuters
Iran war puts Middle East Dubai oil benchmark under stress as prices soar
Kenya tea exports hit by Iran conflict as stocks pile up
Oil slides after Trump says US will end its war on Iran soon
OPEC oil output plunges in March as war forces export cuts, Reuters survey finds
